disclaimer: This directory lists organizations that work with people reconnecting to Indigenous heritage. Inclusion in this directory does not constitute endorsement. Resource availability, contact information, and program scope change over time. Verify directly with each organization before acting on information here. All URLs listed are public-facing organizational websites — no personal contact information is included.
lastReviewNote: Resources reviewed for active status as of early 2026. Website availability should be verified before referencing in materials distributed to people in crisis or seeking urgent support.

categories.title: Sixties Scoop and Adoption Support
categories.description: Organizations specifically focused on Sixties Scoop survivors, their families, and people navigating adoption-related disconnection.

resources.name: Sixties Scoop Network
resources.description: National support network for Sixties Scoop survivors and families. Peer support, community connection, advocacy. Canada-based but serves cross-border families.

resources.name: National Indian Child Welfare Association (NICWA)
resources.description: US-based organization dedicated to the well-being of Native children and families. Programs include foster care, adoption, and family preservation support. ICWA (Indian Child Welfare Act) expertise.

resources.name: First Nations Child and Family Caring Society
resources.description: Canadian organization advocating for First Nations children and families. Research, education, and advocacy on child welfare equity. Resources on reconnection and cultural identity.

categories.title: Tribal Enrollment and Nation Citizenship
categories.description: Starting points for investigating formal tribal citizenship. Each nation determines its own citizenship criteria — these resources help you find the right contact for your specific nation of ancestry.

resources.name: National Congress of American Indians (NCAI)
resources.description: Advocacy organization representing US tribes. Tribal directory can help you locate enrollment offices for specific nations. Does not handle enrollment directly.

resources.name: Assembly of First Nations (AFN)
resources.description: National organization representing First Nations in Canada. Nation directory and contact resources for Canadian First Nations enrollment inquiries.

resources.name: Bureau of Indian Affairs Tribal Leaders Directory
resources.description: US federal directory of federally recognized tribes with contact information. Use to locate the enrollment office for a specific US tribe.

categories.title: Cultural Centers and Museums
categories.description: Places where PNW Coast and First Nations culture is accessible for learning. Many cultural centers welcome people investigating their heritage ancestry, especially when approached with the protocol phrasing (see terminology guide). Call or email before visiting — some require appointments for archival or enrollment inquiries.

resources.name: U'mista Cultural Centre (Alert Bay)
resources.description: Kwakwaka'wakw cultural center. Houses repatriated potlatch regalia. Publicly accessible for educational visits. Kwakwaka'wakw ancestry inquiries: contact directly.

resources.name: Tulalip Tribes Cultural Resources
resources.description: Tulalip Tribes official website. Cultural heritage resources, language programs, and general community information. Puget Sound Coast Salish ancestry.

resources.name: Squamish Nation
resources.description: Squamish Nation official website. Language and cultural learning resources. Sḵwx̱wú7mesh ancestry inquiries.

resources.name: Stó:lō Nation
resources.description: Stó:lō Nation website. Lower Fraser River First Nations cultural resources and community information.

categories.title: Language Learning Starting Points
categories.description: Language programs that welcome learners, including people with heritage ancestry who did not grow up speaking the language. Language learning is one concrete way to begin cultural reconnection that does not require enrollment documentation. Approach with humility: language is community and relationship, not just vocabulary.

resources.name: FirstVoices
resources.description: Community-led language archives for First Nations, Métis, and Inuit languages. Many communities have published public-facing learning materials through FirstVoices. Search by language to find available resources.

resources.name: Endangered Language Fund
resources.description: Supports documentation and revitalization of endangered languages. Directory of language programs may help locate community-specific programs.

categories.title: Genealogical Research Resources
categories.description: Tools for ancestry documentation research. These resources help you build the documentary foundation for your investigation. They do not speak to cultural belonging or enrollment — that comes from community.

resources.name: FamilySearch (LDS)
resources.description: Free genealogy database. Includes Indian census rolls (1885-1940), vital records, and immigration records relevant to Indigenous ancestry research.

resources.name: Library and Archives Canada — First Nations Resources
resources.description: Canadian national archive resources for First Nations genealogy. Indian Register, annuity records, band council records.

securityNote: This directory contains only public organizational URLs. No personal contact information (email addresses, phone numbers of individuals) is included. If you are in crisis related to Sixties Scoop or adoption trauma, contact the Sixties Scoop Network or NICWA for immediate peer support.
